intent: A unified clinical workspace designed to streamline patient lifecycle management by bridging the gap between clinical intake and physician decision-making. The system facilitates role-based access where nurses record vital signs and triage notes, which then automatically trigger actionable insights for doctors to conduct consultations, finalize electronic health records (EHR), and issue secure digital prescriptions in a single workflow.

entities.name: ClinicalEncounterSession
entities.description: An immutable state container capturing the lifecycle of a patient visit, linking patient identification, timestamps, and active session context.

entities.name: TriageObservationRecord
entities.description: A high-fidelity data structure storing vital signs and nurse-recorded triage notes, utilizing offline-first synchronization protocols.

entities.name: DiagnosticFHIRResource
entities.description: An abstraction layer that maps incoming data from heterogeneous HL7/FHIR lab databases into a normalized, queryable internal schema.

entities.name: PhysicianDecisionSupportEngine
entities.description: A rule-based service that processes triage data and laboratory results to generate context-aware clinical insights for physician review.

entities.name: EHRFinalizationManifest
entities.description: A persistent audit-ready object containing the finalized clinical notes, diagnoses, and medical coding necessary for permanent health record updates.

entities.name: SecureDigitalPrescriptionToken
entities.description: An encrypted payload generated through an authenticated API gateway, representing a signed, verifiable order submitted to national e-prescribing networks.

entities.name: IdentityAuthenticationContext
entities.description: A secure middleware service managing MFA-verified roles and granular access permissions for clinical staff, ensuring HIPAA/GDPR compliance.

architecture.description: The system employs an event-driven microservices architecture. Upon scanning a patient card, the ClinicalEncounterSession initializes, pushing local TriageObservationRecord updates to an offline-first caching layer that syncs via WebSockets once connectivity is restored. As nurses input vitals, the PhysicianDecisionSupportEngine monitors the stream, fetching external data via the DiagnosticFHIRResource adaptor to surface actionable insights. Physicians consume these through a unified workspace dashboard. Upon approval, the workflow triggers the EHRFinalizationManifest for record persistence, while simultaneously dispatching a SecureDigitalPrescriptionToken via the API gateway. All interactions are gated by the IdentityAuthenticationContext, ensuring every state transition is logged with cryptographic proof of authorship.

validation_rules: patient_id must be a valid UUID

endpoints.summary: Record triage observations for a session
validation_rules: vital_signs must be a valid JSON object

endpoints.summary: Retrieve clinical insights from the PhysicianDecisionSupportEngine
Finalize EHR manifest and generate audit signature
validation_rules: medical_coding must be a valid JSON schema
encounter_id must be in an active state

rules.logic: An encounter session cannot be modified once initialized. Any attempt to update the header information after the session status is 'active' must be rejected.
Vital signs must fall within physiological bounds; values outside thresholds require an explicit clinical override note before synchronization to the primary database.
The decision support engine shall only generate insights when both a valid TriageObservationRecord and a fully normalized DiagnosticFHIRResource set are present for the active session.
The finalization manifest must be signed by an authorized physician verified through the IdentityAuthenticationContext; non-clinical roles are restricted from finalizing the manifest.
A prescription token cannot be generated unless the EHRFinalizationManifest has been successfully committed to the audit-ready storage layer.

rules.description: IdentityAuthenticationContext session expiry and role validation
rules.logic: Access to write or modify medical records is revoked immediately upon user role change or token expiration; any active transaction must be terminated if authentication is lost.
